prompt: Fix Telegram plugin "extension entry escapes package directory: ./index.ts" error during config validation, then bring in upstream fixes from https://github.com/miadisabelle/mia-openclaw. The upstream renames openclaw→avaclaw (our fork), restructures plugin SDK imports, adds rate limiting for webhooks, fixes token resolution for binding-created accountIds, and adds new module entry points. Work should be done in two phases: (1) fix Telegram + bump/publish patch, then (2) sync remaining upstream changes.

primary.target: Telegram plugin 'extension entry escapes package directory: ./index.ts' error during config validation

secondary.target: openclaw→avaclaw references in synced upstream code
Telegram plugin loads without entry escape error after fix
pnpm build and pnpm check after upstream sync

files_needed: src/plugin-sdk or equivalent SDK import paths
assumptions: upstream repo miadisabelle/mia-openclaw is publicly accessible
'our fork' means avadisabelle/ava-claw is a fork of mia-openclaw/openclaw
the openclaw→avaclaw rename in upstream is analogous to our own rebrand
phase 1 patch publish must happen before phase 2 to not bundle breaking changes with a patch
./index.ts as extension entry is the root cause of the escape error

updates: any plugin SDK import files touched by upstream sync
communications: potentially a PR or commit for phase 2 upstream sync

east.text: Understand why './index.ts' triggers 'escapes package directory' — likely package.json exports or main field resolves to a path outside dist/
Envision a two-phase delivery: isolated Telegram patch first, broader upstream sync second to avoid mixing fixes
Clarify what 'new module entry points' from upstream means in context of our plugin SDK

south.text: Read extensions/telegram/package.json to identify the offending entry field (main, exports, or avaclaw plugin manifest)
Fetch and diff miadisabelle/mia-openclaw against current main to enumerate all upstream changes
Identify which upstream changes are rename-only vs behavioral (rate limiting, token resolution, entry points)
Check if SDK import restructuring affects other extensions beyond Telegram

west.text: Verify Telegram plugin config validation passes after entry fix (no escape error)
Run pnpm build + pnpm check after phase 1 fix
Confirm token resolution fix does not regress existing accountId flows
Validate rate limiting behavior is consistent with existing webhook handling patterns
Confirm npm patch publish succeeds and dist-tag lands correctly

north.text: Cherry-pick or apply upstream openclaw→avaclaw renames (phase 2)
Apply upstream plugin SDK import restructuring (phase 2)
Apply upstream rate limiting for webhooks (phase 2)
Apply upstream token resolution fix for binding-created accountIds (phase 2)
Apply upstream new module entry points (phase 2)

actionStack.text: Read extensions/telegram/package.json and identify the entry field causing escape error
Fix the offending entry in extensions/telegram/package.json (or index.ts path)
Run pnpm build + pnpm check to verify phase 1 fix
Bump patch version in package.json and CHANGELOG.md
Fetch and diff miadisabelle/mia-openclaw to enumerate all upstream changes
Apply plugin SDK import restructuring from upstream
Apply rate limiting for webhooks from upstream
Apply token resolution fix for binding-created accountIds from upstream
Apply new module entry points from upstream
Run pnpm build + pnpm check + pnpm test after full phase 2 sync

ambiguities.text: './index.ts' as the problematic entry — unclear if fix is in package.json exports/main field, plugin manifest, or file relocation
ambiguities.suggestion: Read extensions/telegram/package.json exports/main and the avaclaw plugin entry config to pinpoint exact field

ambiguities.text: 'bring in upstream fixes' — unclear if this means cherry-pick commits, manual apply, or full merge
ambiguities.suggestion: Fetch upstream repo, diff against main, and apply selectively to avoid pulling unrelated changes

ambiguities.text: 'new module entry points' — scope unclear (new npm exports conditions? new plugin capability registrations?)
ambiguities.suggestion: Read upstream commits touching package.json exports and plugin registration to determine scope

ambiguities.text: 'bump/publish patch' — unclear if this is core avaclaw or just the telegram extension package
ambiguities.suggestion: Check if extensions/telegram is published separately as ava-claw-telegram or bundled in core

ambiguities.text: Phase boundary: which upstream changes qualify as 'remaining' for phase 2 vs any that touch Telegram specifically
ambiguities.suggestion: Classify each upstream change by component touched; Telegram-specific fixes could be included in phase 1 if they unblock the entry error
